title: Master's Degree Candidates' Ceremony
description: Celebrate with our graduate students as they complete their advanced degrees and embark on the next chapter.
longDescription: The morning ceremony of CCA's 119th Commencement honors our graduate Class of 2026 — scholars, researchers, and practitioners who have pushed the boundaries of art, design, architecture, and writing at the highest level. This celebration recognizes their dedication to advanced study and the impact their work will have on communities worldwide.

image.alt: Graduates in academic regalia celebrate in a courtyard, some wearing colorful leis and sashes, with historic buildings featuring arched architecture surrounding them.

studentSpeaker.name: Jordan Rivera
studentSpeaker.speechHighlights: Jordan will speak on the role of speculative practice in shaping resilient futures, drawing on their thesis research in climate fiction.

programItems.label: Grand Marshal
programItems.presenter: Professor Elena Vasquez, Professor Emerita of Sculpture

ceremonyProgram.landAcknowledgment: California College of the Arts acknowledges that our campus is located on the unceded territory of Chochenyo and Karkin Ohlone peoples, who have stewarded this land throughout the generations. We recognize their continuing connection to the land and pay our respects to their elders past and present.
ceremonyProgram.disclaimer: The list of degree candidates represents those who have applied for graduation. Inclusion in the commencement program does not confirm that a degree has been conferred. Degrees are officially conferred only upon completion of all academic requirements as certified by the Registrar.
